title: Encryption Key
description: Information about the secret encryption key used to encrypt buffer data.

id.description: A unique identifier for the encryption key. The value must uniquely identify the key within the system. The key ID should be generated to guarantee uniqueness (for example, using a UUID, a random string, a systematically generated id with namespace context, or a cryptographic hash of the key material). It must never contain the key itself or any reversible encoding of the key.

data.description: The secret key encrypted by the user key. When decrypted, this key is used to decrypt buffer data. Standard Base64 (RFC 4648 §4); padding is mandatory when needed.

algorithm.description: The algorithm used to encrypt the secret key.

iv.description: The initialization vector used for encryption of the secret key. Required for encryption methods like AES-GCM. Should be 12 bytes. Standard Base64 (RFC 4648 §4); padding is mandatory when needed.

authTag.description: The authentication tag for verification of the encrypted secret key. Provides integrity verification to encryption modes like AES-GCM. Should be 16 bytes. Standard Base64 (RFC 4648 §4); padding is mandatory when needed.
